Scenario.title: Maroochy Water Breach Scenario
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex This scenario outlines the Maroochy Water Breach, a complex cyber attack targeting the healthcare and public health sector. The attackers employ sophisticated tactics to gain initial access, maintain persistence, and ultimately disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Device theft, Weak passwords, Improper configuration, Vulnerability, Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Obsolete products, Injection flaws, Custom APIs, Autonomous system numbers(ASNs), SSL Certificates and Attribution, WHOIS records, contacts and history, TCP/IP ports, Public and private cloud, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Malicious insiders, Social engineering, Insufficient physical security controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: External/Removable media, Loss or Theft of equipment, Attrition, Web, Account takeover, Inadequate encryption, Browser-based attack, Application compromise, Open port, Vulnerability exploit, Ransomware, Malware, Man in The Middle(MiTM), Session hijacking, Brute-force attack, Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS), Watering-hole, Compromised and weak credentials, SIM Swap, Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities, Web forms, IP address and IP blocks, Domains and subdomains, Botnet, Credential compromise, Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS), Replay Attack, Cryptanalysis Attacks, Side Channel Attacks, Hardware Trojans, Covert Attacks, Sleep Deprivation Attack, Clock Skewing, Attacks Targeting System Reputation, Data and Command Integrity Attack, Eavesdropping, Command and Control Attacks, Phishing, Impersonations/Spoofing, Improper usage, Dumpster diving, Advanced Persistent Threat(APT), Baiting, Shoulder surfing, Reverse social engineering, Business Email Compromise(BEC), Advanced Fee, Extortion

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send spear-phishing emails to specific employees with tailored content to trick them into divulging sensitive information or clicking on malicious links.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers compromise employee devices through drive-by downloads from compromised websites.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers inject malicious code into legitimate processes to execute malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers modify existing processes to inject malicious code without being detected.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to run at regular intervals to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers modify registry keys to ensure malware runs on startup.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to escalate privileges.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers use elevation control mechanisms to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers disable or tamper with anti-virus software to evade detection.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers tamper with system firmware to evade detection and maintain persistence.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers steal credentials to access sensitive areas of the network.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers capture keystrokes to obtain login credentials.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan the network to identify available services and potential targets.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the system, including installed software and configuration.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers use remote services to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers use valid accounts to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect data from the compromised system.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect data from shared drives and network resources.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers transfer stolen data out of the network using FTP.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers use web shells to exfiltrate data.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt critical systems to cause maximum impact.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers access and manipulate critical data to cause maximum damage.

Scenario.summary: This complex attack scenario demonstrates the Maroochy Water Breach, where the attackers employ multiple tactics to gain initial access, maintain persistence, and disrupt operations in the healthcare and public health sector.
